query: I want to add a 'reserve book' feature to our Library NeoHaskell app. We already have a domain overview in event-model.json but haven't modeled this flow yet. Walk me through the full pipeline end to end — from augmenting the request, through event modeling, all the way to green hurl tests.

Starting a brand new NeoHaskell project for a Stock trading platform. Need to model the domain and get the first feature — PlaceOrder — built and tested end to end. What's the correct process and in what order do I run things?

There's a bug in the PlaceOrder command that's already deployed to prod and the file is locked. neo build is rejecting any edits to Commands/PlaceOrder.hs. How do I fix this without touching the locked original?
implement checkout for the cart service

Our event-sourced Haskell app uses the Core prelude (not vanilla Haskell). We need to add an 'add item to wishlist' feature. I don't know which skills to use or in what order — where do I even start and how do all the pieces fit together?

We keep arguing about the right order when adding features to our NeoHaskell CQRS service. Should we write tests before or after event modeling? Should we wire before or after implementing the command handler? Can you lay out the authoritative outside-in pipeline so the whole team follows the same sequence?

Need to add a ReserveStock flow to /home/nick/repos/stockapp — it's a NeoHaskell app. The slice involves a new command, a StockReserved event, an entity update, and an outbound integration to the warehouse service. What's the full sequence from design through green neo test?
build a members-can-borrow-books feature for the library service (neohaskell, event sourced). theres no domain overveiw yet and its not modeled. just drive the whole thing

Help me run the seven-step event modeling process for the 'place order' slice in our NeoHaskell app. I already have the Feature Brief from augment-feature-request. Now I need to produce the event-model.json submodel with chapters, slices, and command/event/query nodes — following the four patterns.

Please review the PR for the AddToCart feature we just implemented in our NeoHaskell app. Check the Decider, StockUpdated event handler, entity update logic, and the wire-feature registration for correctness and idiomatic NeoHaskell style.

I have red unit tests and a red hurl e2e for the PlaceOrder slice — event-model.json is verified and domain types are in place. Now I need to implement the minimal PlaceOrder Decider to make the unit tests go green. Walk me through implement-command.

The ReserveBook command and StockReserved event are implemented, unit tests pass, and acceptance tests pass. The last thing left is to register the endpoint so the outer hurl e2e goes green. How do I wire this into the NeoHaskell router and app entrypoint?

I need to write a unit test for the AddToCart Decider in our NeoHaskell app. Single assertion, given/when/then structure, Hspec, one test per file. The domain types are already in place and I just need the inner RED test before I implement anything.

The BorrowBook slice needs a dueDate field on the BookCopy entity that doesn't exist yet. The entity is add-only so I can't rename or remove existing fields. Walk me through how to safely add this field before I implement the slice.

I have red tests for the ReserveStock slice and now need to create the domain types — StockReservation, ReservationId, ReservationStatus ADT — to make illegal states unrepresentable. Bodies should be stubbed so it compiles but still fails on the assertion. How do I do the domain modeling step?

Turn this vague request into a concrete Feature Brief for our NeoHaskell Library app: 'members should be able to renew their loans online'. Map it to the existing bounded contexts, surface assumptions, identify missing details I need to answer, and produce a structured brief that is ready to hand to event modeling.
